title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Pemberton and Kain discuss a well-planned killing, noting that the location, Monkswell Manor, is likely a specific house in Hapleydon, Berkshire, which Kain identifies from a newspaper crossword puzzle. Pemberton contacts the Berkshire Police Department.

At Monkswell Manor, which has become a hotel, Major Metcalf arrives with two heavy leather trunks. He is a stiff, middle-aged man with a military background in India and is a cousin of Mrs. Boyle. The innkeepers, Jeeves and Molly, are busy with service when a blizzard begins. During the night, a bearded stranger named Paravicini arrives after his car is flipped by snowdrifts. He is a foreigner with a powerful accent and a wallet full of money. Jeeves settles him into the green room. Giles warns that the heavy snow will soon leave them snowed in and cut off from the outside world, lacking deliveries and potentially telephone service.

The next morning, five feet of snow has accumulated. Mrs. Boyle eats breakfast alone, feeling dissatisfied with the establishment. She had sought a social environment to replace the busy, organized life she led during her wartime work, which ended when her house was requisitioned by the military. She finds the high quality of the food and service annoying because it prevents her from complaining. After breakfast, she encounters a red-haired young man with a mocking gaze, whom she deems mentally abnormal. She moves to the living room to claim a rose-colored chair and expresses her displeasure regarding the lukewarm radiator. As she watches snow slide off the roof, she loudly declares she will not stay long, prompting a soft chuckle from a young man named Reen.
